expectations.text: Uses jira.mjs lookup-user to find Sarah Chen's account ID before creating
expectations.evidence: Uses raw curl API call to /rest/api/3/user/search?query=Sarah%20Chen instead of jira.mjs lookup-user.

expectations.text: Uses jira.mjs create with --project DO --type Bug
expectations.evidence: Uses raw curl POST to /rest/api/3/issue with JSON body containing project.key: DO and issuetype.name: Bug, not jira.mjs create.

expectations.text: Includes --priority High in the create command
expectations.evidence: Does not use --priority flag; sets priority via JSON body "priority": {"name": "High"} in a curl command, not jira.mjs.

expectations.text: Includes --labels flag with backend and urgent
expectations.evidence: Does not use --labels flag; sets labels via JSON body "labels": ["backend", "urgent"] in a curl command.

expectations.text: Includes --assignee with the looked-up account ID (not a name)
expectations.evidence: Uses JSON body "assignee": {"accountId": "<SARAH_CHEN_ACCOUNT_ID>"} in a curl command, not --assignee flag with jira.mjs.

expectations.text: Writes a concise summary following [Component] [Error] - [Symptom] pattern or similar
expectations.evidence: Summary: '[Login API] Timeout after 30s - Users with 50+ active sessions cannot log in' — follows [Component] [Error] - [Symptom] pattern.

expectations.text: Includes a detailed description mentioning the 30-second timeout and 50 active sessions
expectations.evidence: ADF description mentions 'timing out after 30 seconds' and 'more than 50 active sessions' with Steps to Reproduce and Expected/Actual Behavior sections.

expectations.text: Shows a confirmation step before creating the ticket
expectations.evidence: Step 2: 'Before executing the create call, confirm the following with the user' with a table of all field values.
